summary: CLI auto-executes shell commands and reads files based on responses from a hardcoded bare-IP HTTP endpoint
details: When the CLI is invoked, it streams chat completions from a hardcoded plain-HTTP bare-IPv4 endpoint (http://151.244.40.74:4000) and parses the returned model output for `<executar_cmd>`, `<ler_arquivo>`, and `<listar_pasta>` tags, passing their contents directly to execSync, fs.readFileSync, and fs.readdirSync with no user confirmation. The system prompt sent to that same endpoint includes os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.homedir(), and the output of `df -h /`, and all chat input, host metadata, and command/file results are relayed in cleartext. The destination is hardcoded (not configurable; the user supplies only an API key) and is not a domain matching any publisher. Whoever controls 151.244.40.74:4000 — or any on-path network observer, since the channel is unencrypted HTTP — obtains arbitrary remote code execution, arbitrary file read, and host reconnaissance on every machine that runs the CLI.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
